title: FFPE sections SP3 digestion -rapizyme and ACN
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7cfd8","text":"This procedure takes as input Formalin-Fixed Paraffin-Embedded (FFPE) samples, typically 2 to 5 slices of 5μmx5mmx5mm. It begins with lysis/deparaffinization of the samples including an ultrasonication step, followed by protein clean-up with sp3-beads, denaturation, reduction and alkylation of cysteines, followed by digestion of proteins to peptides by trypsin (rapizyme from Waters), while using 10% ACN instead of urea during digestion, and ends with peptide level sp3 cleanup.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Preparing 1M Tris-HCl pH8 Prepare 10 mL of 1 Molar (M) aqueous solution of Tris-base. Weigh approximately 1.211 g of Trizma ‱ base powder put in 10 mL Volumetric flask. Fill the flask up to the etched ring mark. Mix thoroughly until dissolved. Calibrate pH meter. Adjust the pH of the solution to pH 8 using HCl solution. Alternatively, use premade TRIS buffer pH 8.0 1 Molar (M) from Merck.

Alkylation reagent preparation Prepare 5 mL of 750 millimolar (mM) aqueous solution of chloroacetamide (CAA). weigh approximately 350.6 mg of the microspatula of CAA powder. add the exact volume of water to a final concentration of 750 millimolar (mM) mix thoroughly (vortex) until dissolved pulse-spin in a microcentrifuge Example n = 350.662 mg/93.51 mg/mmol ≈ 3.75 mmol dissolving in 5 ml of water gives the desired 750 mM concentration. The volumes of reagents can be adjusted to the number of samples.
SP3-bead slurry preparation (sufficient for 10 samples)
Take out the two bottles with Sera-Mag speed beads from the fridge and keep them at room temperature for 10 minutes. 00:10:00
Shake the two SP3 bead bottles gently (without vortex, until all beads are suspended in liquid, takes about 5 minutes). 00:05:00
Combine 50 µL of each bead type in a clean Safe-lock Eppendorf tube (1.5mL or 2mL).

Wash the beads: Place the tube with the bead mixture on a magnetic rack and let the beads settle for 2 minutes. 00:02:00 Remove and discard the supernatant. Rinse the beads with 500 µL water by gentle pipette mixing (off the magnetic rack). Repeat wash steps two more times. Re-suspend and store the beads in 500 µL water in 4 °C The washed beads can be stored in +4ºC up to two weeks. Beads can handle low and high pH, and can be safely heated up to 60ºC. !!! Never freeze the beads. !!! Never sonicate the beads.

Preparing 1M CaCl2 Prepare 1 mL of 1 Molar (M) aqueous solution of calcium chloride hexahydrate (CaCl2). weigh approximately 219 mg of calcium chloride hexahydrate (CaCl2) powder. add the exact volume of water to a final concentration of 1 Molar (M) mix thoroughly (vortex) until dissolved pulse-spin in a microcentrifuge You can aliquot the solution and keep them in freezer.

Digestion Buffer preparation Final buffer concentration: 50 millimolar (mM), HEPES pH 7.8 , 10 millimolar (mM), CaCl2 , 10 % (v/v), ACN ) To have the above final concentration in 1 mL volume, use the following volumes and fill up to 1 mL with water: 50 µL, HEPES 1M pH 7,8 100 µL, ACN 10 µL, 1M CaCl2

Add 200 µL of lysis buffer to each FFPE sample. This protocol is originally written for use with the Twitter Ultrasonic processor from Hielscher, UP200ST . If a Covaris AFA ultrasonicator is available that is also a good option. Bear in mind that optimal sample capacities are different: Covaris sonication will have a capacity of 100μl of lysate maximum.Hielscher sonication can take up to 1mL if necessary. However, 200μl is recommended as maximum due to volume constraints in subsequent steps of the protocol. (see steps 16 and 18 below). As such, for Covaris, a good size of FFPE sample input is 2 slices of 5μmx5mmx5mm. (about 10μg to 20μg of protein). For Hielscher, sample input of 5 such slices is recommended. (about 25μg to 50μg of protein).
Be sure every sample is submerged in the lysis buffer, otherwise vortex or shake them.

Add 300 µL, 750 mM chloroacetamide to each sample, vortex, and incubate for 00:10:00 (10min) at Room temperature , keeping the samples in the fume hood. The volume of 300 μl here is expecting the recommended lysate volume of 200μl (see step 7 above). If the lysate volume is different, the needed volume of chloroacetamide solution to be added should be adjusted accordingly. μμ
Add 50 µL of beads suspension to each sample and pipette gently to resuspend them.

Add 1500 µL, ACN to the samples (acetonitrile concentration of 73%). Concerning volumes, given that the maximum volume capacity of the Eppendorf tube is 2mL, consider the volumes used in steps 7 and 16.
Incubate the suspension in a rotating rack for 00:20:00 (20 min) at Room temperature .
At the end of the incubation remove the tubes from the rack, shake them to detach the beads from the lid and incubate in a magnetic rack for 00:02:00 (2 min).
Aspirate and discard the supernatant, without removing the tube from the rack.
While maintaining the tubes on the magnetic rack, flush the beads with 500 µL, Ethanol 70% and incubate 00:00:30 (30s), then aspirate the liquid phase and discard it.
Repeat the previous step.
While maintaining the tubes on the magnetic rack, flush the beads with 500 µL, ACN , incubate 00:00:30 (30s), aspirate and discard the supernatant.
Let the beads dry for 00:00:20 (20s), then close the lid to avoid excessivedrying.

Dissolve RAPIZYME powder ( 100 µg of trypsin) in 100 µL of Fisher Optima water (quick vortex or pipette up and down). Add the 100 µL of trypsin solution to 1900 µL of digestion buffer. If the entire 100 μg of trypsin are to be used up in one experiment, then one can directly dissolve in the digestion buffer. Otherwise, leftover Rapizyme dissolved in water can be kept in the -20C freezer and be used later (up to a month). Beware of activity degradation due to freeze/thaw cycles, so ideally plan ahead and freeze in aliquots to be used up in single occasions.
Incubate for 16:00:00 (16h) at 37 °C with mild shaking, using an oven type incubator (i.e. avoid temperature gradient between the bottom and the top of the tube).

Lysis Buffer preparation Final buffer concentration: 4%SDS, 0.2 Molar (M), Tris-HCL pH 8 , 0.2 Molar (M), DTT To have the above final concentration in 1 mL volume, use the following volumes and fill up to 1 mL with water: 200 µL, SDS 20% 200 µL, DTT 1M 200 µL, Tris-HCl 1M pH 8
Heat the samples in the heating block at 95 °C for 00:30:00 (30min) in agitation, 400 rpm.
Remove the samples from the heating block and let it cool down for some minutes at Room temperature .

Spin down the samples and Sonicate the samples with Twitter sonicator with the following settings: Set the Amplitude (A) to 100% Set the pulsation mode (C) to 100% Period Clock : On On Time : 60s Off Time : 30s Limit Type : Time Limit Value : To perform 10 cycles you need to set 00:15:00 (15min) . Calculating Limit Value : (60s On Time + 30 Off Time) * Number of Cycles For example: For 10 Cycles: (60+30)*10= 900s = You should set 15 min on instrument. IMPORTANT: Ensure all positions on the Twitter sonicator are occupied. If you do not have enough samples to fill the positions, use tubes filled with water to balance the instrument.
Heat the samples in the heating block at 95 °C for 00:15:00 (15min) in agitation, 400 rpm.

Spin down the samples and Sonicate the samples with twitter with the following settings: Set the Amplitude (A) to 100% Set the pulsation mode (C) to 100% Period Clock : On On Time : 60s Off Time : 30s Limit Type : Time Limit Value : To perform 5 cycles you need to set 00:07:30 . IMPORTANT: Ensure all positions on the Twitter sonicator are occupied. If you do not have enough samples to fill the positions, use tubes filled with water to balance the instrument.
Centrifuge at 13000 x g, 23 °C, for 00:10:00 (10 min) at Room temperature .
Recover the supernatant and put in a new Safe-lock 2mL Eppendorf tube.

After overnight incubation, add 2 mL, acetonitrile to reach a concentration of more than 95 % (v/v) ACN. In order guarantee the binding of all peptides to the SP3 beads, the organic content composition of the liquid must be >95%.
Incubate the tubes in a gently rotating rack for 20min 00:20:00 at Room temperature .
After incubation, shake the tubes to recover the beads stuck on the lid, then incubate the tubes on a magnetic rack for 00:02:00 (2 min).
While maintaining the tubes on the magnetic rack, aspirate and discard the liquid phase, then flush the beads with 500 µL, acetonitrile and wait for 00:00:30 (30s).
Repeat the previous step.
While maintaining the tubes on the magnetic rack, aspirate and discard the liquid phase, then let it dry a few seconds.
Incubate the tube on the magnetic rack for 00:02:00 (2 min), then recover the liquid phase and put it in a new Low-bind 1.5mL Eppendorf tube.
Repeat the previous step, to be sure of removing all the beads.
Perform the peptide concentration measurement, using Optima LCMS grade water as blank.
Take the tubes off the magnetic rack, and resuspend in 50 µL of MS grade water. Mix gently by brief vortex or pipetting and spin down.
Add 100 µL from the trypsin containing digestion buffer to each sample (i.e. onto the dry beads).
